# Asset fixtures

Real files for the loading paths that in-memory tests cannot reach:
`loadTexture`/`loadAtlas` in `src/render/atlas.ts`, and the `url` branch of
`Audio` in `src/audio/index.ts`. Everything else the renderer and audio are
tested against is generated at runtime — a canvas texture or a synthesised
buffer — so decoding, colour space, alpha and dimension arithmetic have never
actually run.

Generated by `bun tools/make-fixtures.ts`, and **committed on purpose**: a test
that regenerates its own inputs proves less than one with fixed inputs, because
an encoder and a decoder that drift together both stay green.

These are not upstream's PNGs, for two reasons. Upstream art is Touhou
derivative and cannot ship (CLAUDE.md rule 9), and every upstream sheet is
power-of-two with clean alpha — so it would exercise only the cases that already
work. Each file below is aimed at a specific way a loader breaks. **Do not
delete the odd-looking ones; the odd shapes are the point.**

| File | What it is | The failure it catches |
|---|---|---|
| `grid-8x2.png` | 256x64 RGBA, a 32x32 grid matching `BULLET_GRID`, 16 flat distinct colours | UV or cell-addressing errors. A wrong cell shows as plainly the wrong colour rather than as something subtly off |
| `npot.png` | 100x60 RGBA, RGB ramps plus an alpha ramp across x | Any lingering power-of-two assumption. WebGL1 needed POT for wrapping and mipmaps; WebGL2 does not. The alpha ramp also catches a dropped or transposed alpha channel |
| `no-alpha.png` | 64x48, PNG **colour type 2** — no alpha channel at all | The defect upstream shipped: `rumia.png` had no alpha and rendered on an opaque box. Our loader should treat it as opaque, predictably |
| `ragged.png` | 100x40 RGBA against a 32x32 grid — 3 whole columns, 1 whole row | Grid maths that rounds the cell count up or walks off a row. The 4px right strip and 8px bottom strip belong to no cell and are **magenta**, so an out-of-bounds read shows as colour instead of silence |
| `one-pixel.png` | 1x1 RGBA, half-transparent (255,128,0,128) | The degenerate case, where a loader is most likely to take a shortcut. Partial alpha because 255 is easy to produce by accident |
| `blip.wav` | 44.1kHz 16-bit **mono**, 2646 frames (0.060s) | Basic PCM decode, and a short buffer where an off-by-one in length arithmetic is audible |
| `stereo.wav` | 44.1kHz 16-bit **stereo**, 11025 frames (0.250s) | Channel swap. Left is 220Hz at peak 0.90, right 1320Hz at peak 0.25 — the channels differ in both frequency and level, so a swap is measurable whether a check reads peaks or counts zero crossings |

## Validity

`tools/make-fixtures.ts` re-reads every file after writing it and parses it with
code independent of the encoder: signature, chunk CRCs, IHDR fields, and the
decompressed IDAT checked against the dimensions IHDR declares. It then asserts
the pixels are the ones intended, and confirms the result against the system
decoders (`sips`, `afinfo`) where those exist.

It also **proves that check can fail**, in the manner of
`test/visual/layer-order.ts`: it mutates a copy of `grid-8x2.png` in six
plausible ways and requires every one to be rejected, then requires the
unmutated file to be accepted. One of those mutations writes a wrong width *and*
repairs the CRC over it, which no checksum can catch — only comparing IHDR
against the decompressed pixel count does.
